CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUL-24-2024 CONTINUED TO OCT-23-2024 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT(S) TO BRING CROSS COMPLAINT(S) AT ISSUE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT JACINTO VALDOVINO #AM3654 **PROVIDE ACCESS** DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT JACINTO VALDOVINO'S DEMURRER IS, ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION, CONTINUED TO TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 4, 2025 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 301. PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION/RESPONSE TO DEMURRER MUST BE FILED AND SERVED NO LATER THAN JANUARY 22, 2025, AND DEFENDANT'S REPLY MUST BE FILED AND SERVED NO LATER THAN JANUARY 28, 2025. COURTESY COPIES MUST BE PROVIDED TO DEPARTMENT 301 NO LATER THAN TWENTY-FOUR HOURS AFTER SUBMISSION FOR FILING. THE COURT NOTES THAT ON DECEMBER 4, 2024, IT ISSUED AN ORDER RELIEVING MR. SCHWARTZ AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS. THE ORDER PROVIDES THAT IT WOULD NOT BE EFFECTIVE UNTIL MR. SCHWARTZ FILED A PROOF OF SERVICE OF THE SIGNED ORDER. TO DATE, MR. SHWARTZ HAS NOT FILED A PROOF OF SERVICE AND, THUS, HE REMAINS COUNSEL OF RECORD FOR PLAINTIFFS. CLERK TO PREPARE ORDER TO APPEAR WITH THE COURT'S RULING AS AN ATTACHMENT. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (301/JMQ)

LAW AND MOTION 301, CROSS COMPLAINANT JACINTO VALDOVINO'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. THE COURT ISSUED AN ORDER ON NOVEMBER 20, 2025, STATING ITS INTENTION TO GRANT LEAVE TO CROSS-DEFENDANT JASON DEWITT TO AMEND HIS ANSWER TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. DEWITT HAS ATTEMPTED TO FILE A VERIFIED ANSWER BUT IT APPEARS THAT WILL BE REJECTED BY THE CLERK'S OFFICE BECAUSE IT IS CAPTIONED AN "ANSWER" INSTEAD OF AN "AMENDED ANSWER." THE COURT GRANTS DEWITT LEAVE TO AMEND IN A FORM ACCEPTABLE TO THE CLERK'S OFFICE WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. COURT WILL PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

calendar.matters: CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS COMPLAINANT JACINTO VALDOVINO'S #AM3654 **PROVIDE ACCESS** MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant and cross-complainant Jacinto Jose Valdovino's motion is denied. Valdovino seeks miscellaneous relief, including an opportunity to demur to an answer to a supplemental cross-complaint that he contends was never served on him. The court denies this relief; there is a proof of service attached to the cross-complaint and the court credits it. The court denies Valdovino's request for an order specifying any particular kind of service. The Code of Civil Procedure sets out permissible means of service and the court lacks authority to compel parties to use one kind of permitted service rather than another. The court denies Valdovino's request for an order clarifying and confirming a prior order; the request is an untimely request for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure, section 1008, subdivision (a).

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Pursuant to the court's order to show cause, Jacinto Jose Valdovino's demurrer to Jason Dewitt's answer to Valdovino's cross-complaint is ordered off calendar as mooted by Dewitt's amended answer, filed on December 8, 2025. The court vacates its order of December 12, 2025 granting Dewitt leave to amend; that order was mooted by the filing of the December 8 amended answer.

Valdovino seeks further relief, asking the court to extend his time to demur or otherwise respond to the amended answer because he did not receive service of the amended answer. Any relief not contemplated in the court's order to show cause must be sought by separate motion. The court notes, however, that the December 8 amended answer appears to attach a proper proof of service.

Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

DEMURRER to ANSWER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Donald Charles Schwartz, Esq. Law Offices of Donald C. Schwartz 831-331-9909; donald@lawofficedonaldschwartz.com Attorney for Cross-Defendant Jason Dewitt, Dewitt Detective Agency
Jacinto Jose Valdovino, Pro Per Defendant

Hearing is held. Discussed, not ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT JACINTO VALDOVINO'S DEMURRER to ANSWER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

The court issued an order on November 20, 2025, stating its intention to grant leave to cross-defendant Jason Dewitt to amend his answer to the cross-complaint. Dewitt has attempted to file a verified answer but it appears that will be rejected by the clerk's office because it is captioned an "answer" instead of an "amended answer." The court grants Dewitt leave to amend in a form acceptable to the clerk's office within 20 days of this order.

Court will prepare an order.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-07-2025 Continued

Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT JACINTO VALDOVINO's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion for leave to file a first amended cross-complaint is granted in part and denied in part.

Cross-claimant Jacinto Jose Valdovino seeks to file a first amended cross complaint to (1) allege a malicious prosecution claim against Jason Dewitt, and (2) to attach a verification and exhibits, and correct typographical errors.

Leave to allege a malicious prosecution claim in an amended complaint is denied on a ground not raised by either party. "Matters which occur after the filing of a complaint may not be alleged by amendment to the complaint, but must be brought into the action by means of a supplemental complaint." (Hebert v. Los Angeles Raiders, Ltd. (1991) 23 Cal.App.4th 414, 426 [citing CCP 464].) The cross complaint was filed on March 25, 2024. Any amendment would relate back to that date. One element of a malicious prosecution claim is that the prior action was "was pursued to a legal termination favorable to the" malicious prosecution plaintiff. (Nunez v. Pennisi (2015) 241 Cal.App.4th 861, 872-873 [internal quotation marks and citation omitted].) That element is not alleged to have occurred, and thus Valdovino's claim did not accrue, until after March 25, 2024. (The court takes judicial notice of the papers on file in this case; the court dismissed Dewitt's complaint on February 28, 2025.) Thus, an amended complaint is not the proper vehicle to raise a malicious prosecution claim. A supplemental complaint is a separate kind of filing and leave to file it may be sought by noticed motion.

Valdovino's motion also seeks to attach a verification and exhibits, and correct typos. Valdovino explains that he has limited access to a law library and exhibits. Dewitt opposes on the grounds of futility, contending that res judicata bars all of Valdovino's claims because Valdovino previously prevailed in a small claims action on the same set of facts. The court grants leave to amend to make the ministerial corrections Valdovino seeks. The res judicata arguments may or may not have merit; the court does not decide them here. But the amendment the court gives Valdovino leave to file does no more than restate what is already at issue in this case.

Finally, the court notes that the amended complaint attached to Valdovino's declaration also sets out a breach of fiduciary duty cause of action as a new sixth cause of action. Valdovino's moving papers offer no argument or explanation why this cause of action could not earlier have been alleged. Leave to amend to allege a breach of fiduciary duty cause of action is denied without prejudice.

Any amendment filed pursuant to this order must be filed within 30 days of entry of the order.

Prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT JACINTO VALDOVINO's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The demurrer will be sustained with leave to amend.

To prevail on a claim for assault, Plaintiff must show, among other things, that Defendant threatened to touch him in a harmful or offensive manner, and it appeared to Plaintiff that Defendant was about to carry out that threat. (CACI No. 1301.) Words alone do not amount to an assault. (Id.) Plaintiff must allege that in addition to Defendant intending to cause a harmful or offensive contact, or an imminent apprehension thereof, that Plaintiff was put in imminent apprehension of such harmful or offensive contact. See Lowry v. Standard Oil Co. (1944) 63 Cal.App.2d 1, 6-7.

Plaintiff alleges a third party, not Defendant Valdovino, called him and stated, "You live with your daughter, right?" which Plaintiff construed as an "implied threat to his daughter" a nonparty to the case. (Cmplnt., 4:2-9.) There are no allegations that Defendant Valdovino threatened to touch Plaintiff in a harmful or offensive matter or that there was a reasonable appearance he was about to carry out carry out an "implied" threat. Additionally, there are no allegations Defendant was put in imminent apprehension of such harmful or offensive contact. Consequently, Defendant's demurrer to the assault claim is sustained with leave to amend.

To prevail on a claim for negligence, a plaintiff must show (a) a legal duty to use due care, (b) a breach of such legal duty, and (c) the breach as the proximate or legal cause of the resulting injury. (Ladd v.County of San Mateo (1996) 12 Cal.4th 913, 917.) "Breach is the failure to meet the standard of care." (Coyle v. Historic MissionInn Corp. (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 627, 643.) "A defendant does not owe a legal duty to protect against third party conduct, unless there exists a special relationship between the defendant and the plaintiff. In that circumstance, '[i]n addition to the special relationship . . . , there must also be evidence showing facts from which the trier of fact could reasonably infer that the [defendant] had prior actual knowledge, and thus must have known, of the offender's assaultive propensities. [Citation.]' In short, the third party's misconduct must be foreseeable to the defendant." (Doe v. Los Angeles County Dept. of Children & Family Services (2019) 37 Cal.App.5th 675, 682-683.)

Plaintiff fails to allege any facts to demonstrate that a special relationship existed between him and Defendant. Plaintiff's Opposition does not address the demurrer to the claim of negligence. Thus, Defendant's demurrer to the negligence claim is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's first amended complaint must be filed and served by February 20, 2025.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
